stories.headline: Israel intensifies attacks on southern Lebanon, killing at least 16 people
stories.summary: Southern Lebanon empties out under bombardment as Red Cross warns of worsening crisis.

stories.headline: US and Iran Reach Deal to Extend Truce, Axios Reports
stories.summary: Iran and the US have reached a deal to extend a truce and work toward an agreement to end the war, Axios reported, sending oil prices lower. Tyler Kendall reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Beirut amid increasing escalation in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: The intensification comes after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced an expansion in the Israeli military's attacks in Lebanon, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: US Defense Orders Surge to Near-Record Amid War With Iran
stories.summary: US orders for defense capital goods surged in April to the second-highest level on record, adding to signs that the Iran war is boosting demand for military hardware.

stories.headline: US Stocks Reverse Losses on Iran Deal Report as Oil Pares Gains
stories.summary: Stocks rebounded from earlier losses on Thursday, as investors reacted to a reported deal between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: After stern words from Trump, U.S. and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: The renewed violence came after the president said Iran was wrong if it thought he would feel pressure to make concessions and resolve the standoff before U.S. midterm elections later this year.

stories.headline: EU will never be a neutral mediator between Ukraine and Russia, says Kallas
stories.summary: The High Representative has dismissed the buzz over a special envoy for talks with Russia, arguing the EU will "never" be a neutral mediator between Moscow and Kyiv. "We have been clearly on Ukraine's side," she said.

stories.headline: Israel strikes Beirut and southern Lebanon after large-scale evacuation orders
stories.summary: The Israeli military has ordered Lebanese residents to leave some 17% of the country's territory.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange fire amid increasingly strained truce

stories.headline: Russia on the back foot with dynamics of war shifting in Ukraine’s favour, EU says – as it happened

stories.headline: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll
stories.summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.

stories.headline: Man meets Dutch volunteer caring for father's WW2 grave
stories.summary: A Liverpool man says it was "marvellous" to meet the volunteer who cares for his father's war grave in the Netherlands.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Aluminum Market Tightness Could Continue for 'Long Time,' Morgan Stanley Says
stories.summary: Amy Gower, metals and mining commodities strategist at Morgan Stanley, discusses the impact of the Iran war on aluminum prices. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, she warns the market could "stay quite tight" for a long time. "Most of the smelters that are shut are damaged, and so they're looking at sort of twelve-month restart timelines," Gower explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Earnings are Trumping Everything, Says Bob Doll
stories.summary:
Earnings estimates are surging and the growth of AI spending has served as a counterweight to the hit from Iran.
Bob Doll, CIO at Crossmark Global Investments, joins for a discussion on equity resiliency and warning from the bond vigilantes. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Judge Declines, for Now, to Block Mail-In Voting Changes Ordered by Trump
stories.summary: The ruling allowed the Trump administration to continue to pursue changes that include enlisting the Postal Service in checking voters against a national database.

stories.headline: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
stories.summary: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest in Texas for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton.

stories.headline: Massie Visits Greene in Costa Rica for Fishing and ‘Spicy’ Political Talks
stories.summary: The two Republican critics of President Trump — Thomas Massie, who lost his House primary last week, and Marjorie Taylor Greene, who resigned from Congress — met up in the tropics.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
stories.summary: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.

stories.headline: South African president to face impeachment probe over ‘Farmgate’ scandal

stories.headline: Canada signs landmark LNG energy deal with Germany
stories.summary: The deal would see one million tons of natural gas shipped annually from Canada to Germany by the early 2030s through a proposed terminal in British Columbia.

stories.headline: Copasa Relaunches Share Offering After Bids Came Below Minimum
stories.summary: Cia de Saneamento de Minas Gerais announced new terms for its privatization process, after two bids for a 30% stake in the Brazilian water utility came in below the government’s expectations.

stories.headline: Argentine Provinces Tap Global Markets That Sovereign Avoids
stories.summary: Argentine provinces are tapping global bond markets at the fastest pace in nearly a decade, even as the federal government sticks to short-term debt in the local market amid investor fears over the 2027 elections.

stories.headline: Delfin Is Close to Proceeding With First US Floating LNG Project
stories.summary: Delfin Midstream Inc. is close to greenlighting what would be the first floating liquefied natural gas export site in the US, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: China Sells $885 Million of Green Bonds in Hong Kong Debut
stories.summary: China priced 6 billion yuan ($885 million) of green sovereign bonds on Thursday, a year after the government’s debut in the international ESG debt market.

stories.headline: Goldman's Snider Sees Earnings Driving Stocks Higher
stories.summary: Goldman Sachs Chief US Equity Strategist Ben Snider discusses the recent increase in the firm's S&P 500 price target, highlighting strong earnings growth as the primary driver behind the adjustment. Speaking with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Snider also says he expects AI gains to broaden out in the market. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: EU’s Economic Powers Seek Unity to Push Capital Markets Merger
stories.summary: The European Union’s six largest economies are advancing plans for a bloc-wide capital markets union, hoping their collective voice will spur the continent to action after years of delay.

stories.headline: Why Democrats Think They Can Flip Texas
stories.summary: With Ken Paxton defeating Senator John Cornyn in Texas’ Republican runoff for the Senate, The New York Times’s national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains why Democrats may now see their best chance in years to flip the seat.

stories.headline: Family accuse UK government of lack of support over death of Briton in Grenada

stories.headline: The race for oil: will Jamaica be the next country to drill and what does that mean for its green pledges?

Tests on samples from the seabed off the Caribbean island’s south coast earlier this year identified hydrocarbons, which suggest the presence of crude oil below ground.Jamaica imports all its fuel, which costs about $1.5-2bn (£1.1bn-1.5bn) annually, depending o

stories.headline: UK minister visits Australia for ‘lessons’ ahead of expected British social media crackdown
stories.summary: Kanishka Narayan says Australia’s pioneering law has contributed to national conversation under way in BritainThe UK’s online safety minister says he has spent a week in Australia learning the “practical lessons” of the country’s under-16s social media ban amid concern that many teenagers are bypassing the law.The British government is expected to announce a social media crackdown within weeks after a public consultation that could see the UK fol

stories.headline: ‘Hidden datacentre tax’ costing Irish households millions, report says
stories.summary: Datacentres used 22% of country’s electricity last year, pushing up household bills, study suggestsEnergy demand by datacentres in Ireland has added hundreds of euros to household electricity bills in a pattern that could be replicated across Europe, according to a report.Ireland’s growing number of datacentres last year used 22% of the country’s electricity, more than all urban homes combined, according to the Central Statistics Office. stories.headline: Yemen’s former leader Abd-Rabbu Mansour Hadi dies in exile at 80
stories.summary: Hadi was the internationally recognised president of Yemen who led a fractured government mostly from exile.

stories.headline: Oil Pares Gain on News of Possible US-Iran Pact to Extend Truce
stories.summary: Oil pared gains on a report that the US and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to extend the ceasefire between the two countries and launch negotiations on the Islamic Republic’s nuclear program.

stories.headline: Stocks and Bonds Rise After US-Iran Deal Report: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Wall Street traders sent stocks toward all-time highs, bonds rose while a rally in oil waned on speculation the US and Iran are close to a diplomatic breakthrough in the war that has rattled global markets.

stories.headline: Why some Republicans are worried about Ken Paxton as a Senate nominee
stories.summary: Few politicians have garnered as much scandal in Texas as Paxton, but he has ignited the MAGA base, who see him as a fighter.

stories.headline: Ghana welcomes Pope's apology over Catholic Church's role in slavery
stories.summary: The West African nation was one of the main gateways for the transatlantic slave trade.

stories.headline: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat
stories.summary: Dong Guangping, an activist and former police officer, spent hours at sea before reaching Korean waters.

stories.headline: Spanish police raid HQ of governing Socialists as corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: The searches come amid a series of scandals affecting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's PSOE.

stories.headline: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China

stories.headline: Cornyn’s Defeat in Texas Runoff Underscores Trump's Grip on the G.O.P.
stories.summary: Senator John Cornyn lost to his MAGA-aligned challenger, Ken Paxton, by 28 percentage points. It was a historically poor showing.

stories.headline: Did Trump pick the right blue for the Reflecting Pool? We asked a pool guy.
stories.summary: Old Glory Blue? American Flag Blue? Let's reflect on all the shades, while a federal judge mulls "aesthetic injury" in Trump's latest decorating flourish.

stories.summary: The court said the man poisoned the tycoon for sidelining him after he helped secure a deal with Netflix for the 3 Body Problem.

stories.headline: To repair ties with India, Rubio leans into pageantry

stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: Rosenberg: Luhansk strike sparks Russian accusations and vow to retaliate

stories.headline: From AI to interceptors, Ukraine is trying to drone-proof its skies
stories.summary: After four years of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukraine has become far more successful at fighting off air raids.
